methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mariana Minerals is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Turner Caldwell that has raised $95M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with direct stage and sector matches including Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy base and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yields $190M. Peer-median analysis across three same-stage, same-sector comparables—Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M—produces a median implied value of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. The peer set reflects materiality in the frontier energy sector, including reference to TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials among broader comparable cohorts.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $128M (low) to $218M (high), with a median point of $170M. This range reflects tension between stage-progression methodology, which tends higher, and peer-median positioning, which anchors nearer the lower-to-mid quartile of the climate tech peer set. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, differentiation in core technology, and path to Series B fundraising conditions in current market environments.
